parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XMutex instance to be worked on.
is the device configuration structure containing required HW build data.
is the Physical address of the hardware in a Virtual Memory operating system environment. It is the Base Address in a stand alone environment.

briefdescription.para: Locks a particular Mutex lock within a Mutex device.

detaileddescription.para: Call blocks till the Mutex is locked.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XMutex instance to be worked on.

is the specific Mutex lock within the device to operate on. Each device may contain multiple Mutex locks. The Mutex number is a zero based number with a range of 0 - (InstancePtr->Config.NumMutex - 1).

listitem.para: XMutex_Trylock is a blocking call. This call blocks until the user gets the lock.
Use XMutex_Trylock for a Non-Blocking call. The user gets the lock if it is available and returns immediately if the lock is not available.

briefdescription.para: Locks a particular Mutex lock within a Mutex device.

detaileddescription.para: Call returns immediately if the Mutex is already locked (This is Non-Blocking call).

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XMutex instance to be worked on.

is the specific Mutex lock within the device to operate on. Each device may contain multiple Mutex locks. The Mutex number is a zero based number with a range of 0 - (InstancePtr->Config.NumMutex - 1).

listitem.para: XST_DEVICE_BUSY if the Mutex was found to be already locked
This is Non-Blocking call, the user gets the lock if it is available else XST_DEVICE_BUSY is returned.
Use XMutex_Lock if you need to block until a lock is obtained.

briefdescription.para: Unlocks a particular Mutex lock within a Mutex device.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XMutex instance to be worked on.

is the specific Mutex lock within the device to operate on. Each device may contain multiple Mutex locks. The Mutex number is a zero based number with a range of 0 - (InstancePtr->Config.NumMutex - 1).

para.verbatim: - XST_SUCCESS if locking was successful. - XST_FAILURE if the Mutex was locked by process with different ID.

briefdescription.para: Gets the current lock state of a Mutex lock within a Mutex device.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XMutex instance to be worked on.

is the specific Mutex lock within the device to operate on. Each device may contain multiple Mutex locks. The Mutex number is a zero based number with a range of 0 - (InstancePtr->Config.NumMutex - 1).

briefdescription.para: Gets the current status of a Mutex lock within a Mutex device.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XMutex instance to be worked on.

is the specific Mutex lock within the device to operate on. Each device may contain multiple Mutex locks. The Mutex number is a zero based number with a range of 0 - (InstancePtr->Config.NumMutex - 1).
is a pointer where the current lock status is stored. Sets memory pointed to by 'Locked' to 1 if the Mutex is locked and 0 if it is unlocked.
is a pointer where the current owner status is stored. . If the Mutex is locked, the memory pointed to by 'Owner' is updated to reflect the CPU ID that has currently locked this Mutex.

briefdescription.para: Gets the USER register of a Mutex lock within a Mutex device.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XMutex instance to be worked on.

is the specific Mutex lock within the device to operate on. Each device may contain multiple Mutex locks. The Mutex number is a zero based number with a range of 0 - (InstancePtr->Config.NumMutex - 1).
is a pointer to an u32 where the current user register value is stored by this function.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if successful. Memory pointed to by User is updated to reflect the contents of the user register.
XST_NO_FEATURE if the Mutex was not configured with a USER register.

briefdescription.para: Sets the USER register of a Mutex lock within a Mutex device.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XMutex instance to be worked on.

is the specific Mutex lock within the device to operate on. Each device may contain multiple Mutex locks. The Mutex number is a zero based number with a range of 0 - (InstancePtr->Config.NumMutex - 1).
is the value to update the USER register with.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if the USER register is written with the given value .
XST_NO_FEATURE if the Mutex was not configured with a USER register.

briefdescription.para: Looks up the device configuration based on the unique device ID/BaseAddress.

detaileddescription.para: The XMutex_ConfigTable[] contains the configuration info for each device in the system.

parameterdescription.para: contains the base address of the device

simplesect.para: A pointer to the configuration found or NULL if the specified device ID/BaseAddress was not found. See xmutex.h for the definition of XMutex_Config.
In XSCT/classic flow, DeviceId is used to look up the device configuration.

briefdescription.para: Selftest a particular Mutex hardware core.

detaileddescription.para:
InstancePtr
is a pointer to the XMutex instance to be worked on.
XST_SUCCESS if test was successful.
XST_FAILURE if test was not successful.
This test is destructive. It will fail if the Mutex is currently being used. This is also a blocking call, if there is another process which has the Mutex, the first _lock will hand the test until the other process releases it.
